text: The ‘marinam purpuram’ is a purple dye, or cloths colored with such a dye, made from shellfish and often called Tyrian purple or Royal purple. By today’s standards, this color is actually more of a scarlet hue. The reference to ‘hyacinthinum’ is probably silk or other cloths colored with this blue dye.

The Jewish month of Kislev falls in November/ December in the Christian calendar. In the future, the Church will celebrate Christmas, beginning on November 25th, for eight days. November 25th is the true date of the Birth of Christ.
